---
title: "Why Vulnerability MTTR Alone Misleads"
subtitle: "Add MOVA to Measure Real Risk"
author: "Caleb Kinney"
date: ""
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import pandas as pd
import polars as pl
from great_tables import GT
from plotnine import (
    aes,
    coord_cartesian,
    element_blank,
    element_line,
    element_rect,
    element_text,
    geom_line,
    geom_point,
    geom_text,
    ggplot,
    labs,
    scale_color_manual,
    scale_x_continuous,
    theme,
    theme_minimal,
)

metrics = pl.read_parquet("data/metrics.parquet")
summary = pl.read_parquet("data/summary.parquet")

STRATEGY_LABELS = {
    "oldest_first": "Oldest-First",
    "newest_first": "Newest-First",
}
STRATEGY_COLORS = {
    "Oldest-First": "#1F6F78",
    "Newest-First": "#8B1E3F",
}
ROOM_BG = "#f6f0e5"
PANEL_BG = "#fffaf2"
GRID_COLOR = "#d9cfbf"


def fmt_days(value: float) -> str:
    return f"{value:.1f}"


metrics_labeled = metrics.with_columns(
    pl.col("strategy").replace(STRATEGY_LABELS).alias("strategy_label"),
)

mttr_compare = pd.DataFrame(
    metrics_labeled
    .select(
        pl.col("strategy_label").alias("strategy"),
        "month_index",
        pl.col("mttr_days").alias("days"),
    )
    .to_dicts()
)

mova_compare = pd.DataFrame(
    metrics_labeled
    .select(
        pl.col("strategy_label").alias("strategy"),
        "month_index",
        pl.col("mova_days").alias("days"),
    )
    .to_dicts()
)

tail_plot = pd.DataFrame(
    metrics_labeled
    .select(
        pl.col("strategy_label").alias("strategy"),
        "month_index",
        "aged_over_180",
    )
    .to_dicts()
)

summary_named = summary.with_columns(
    pl.col("strategy").replace(STRATEGY_LABELS).alias("strategy_label"),
)

summary_table = (
    pd.DataFrame(
        summary_named
        .with_columns(
            pl.col("strategy_label").alias("Strategy"),
            pl.col("mttr_days").round(1).alias("MTTR (days)"),
            pl.col("mova_days").round(1).alias("MOVA (days)"),
            pl.col("open_count").alias("Open"),
            pl.col("aged_over_180").alias("180+ days"),
            pl.when(pl.col("strategy") == "newest_first")
            .then(pl.lit(0))
            .otherwise(pl.lit(1))
            .alias("sort_order"),
        )
        .sort("sort_order")
        .select("Strategy", "MTTR (days)", "MOVA (days)", "Open", "180+ days")
        .to_dicts()
    )
)

card_rows = (
    summary_named
    .select(
        "strategy_label",
        pl.col("mttr_days").round(1).alias("mttr_days"),
        pl.col("mova_days").round(1).alias("mova_days"),
        "open_count",
        "aged_over_180",
    )
    .to_dicts()
)

card_lookup = {row["strategy_label"]: row for row in card_rows}
oldest = card_lookup["Oldest-First"]
newest = card_lookup["Newest-First"]

oldest_mttr = fmt_days(oldest["mttr_days"])
oldest_mova = fmt_days(oldest["mova_days"])
oldest_open = int(oldest["open_count"])
oldest_tail = int(oldest["aged_over_180"])

newest_mttr = fmt_days(newest["mttr_days"])
newest_mova = fmt_days(newest["mova_days"])
newest_open = int(newest["open_count"])
newest_tail = int(newest["aged_over_180"])

shared_theme = (
    theme_minimal(base_size=22)
    + theme(
        figure_size=(11.2, 5.15),
        legend_position="none",
        legend_title=element_blank(),
        panel_grid_minor=element_blank(),
        panel_grid_major_x=element_blank(),
        panel_grid_major_y=element_line(color=GRID_COLOR, size=0.55),
        panel_background=element_rect(fill=PANEL_BG, color=None),
        plot_background=element_rect(fill=ROOM_BG, color=None),
        axis_title=element_text(color="#26333A"),
        axis_text=element_text(color="#26333A"),
        plot_title=element_text(weight="bold", color="#101828"),
        plot_subtitle=element_text(color="#55606B", margin={"b": 10}),
        legend_text=element_text(color="#26333A"),
        plot_margin=0.16,
    )
)

chart_theme = shared_theme + theme(
    axis_title_x=element_text(margin={"t": 10}),
    axis_title_y=element_text(margin={"r": 10}),
)


def comparison_chart(source: pd.DataFrame, title: str, subtitle: str, y_label: str):
    labels = source[source["month_index"] == source["month_index"].max()].copy()
    labels["label"] = labels.apply(
        lambda row: f'{row["strategy"]} {row["days"]:.0f}d', axis=1
    )

    return (
        ggplot(source, aes(x="month_index", y="days", color="strategy"))
        + geom_line(size=2.35)
        + geom_point(data=labels, size=3.8)
        + geom_text(
            data=labels,
            mapping=aes(label="label"),
            ha="left",
            nudge_x=0.45,
            size=10,
        )
        + scale_color_manual(values=STRATEGY_COLORS)
        + scale_x_continuous(breaks=[1, 6, 12, 18, 24])
        + coord_cartesian(xlim=(1, 26))
        + labs(title=title, subtitle=subtitle, x="Month", y=y_label)
        + chart_theme
    )


tail_labels = tail_plot[tail_plot["month_index"] == tail_plot["month_index"].max()].copy()
tail_labels["label"] = tail_labels.apply(
    lambda row: f'{row["strategy"]} {row["aged_over_180"]}', axis=1
)
```

## MTTR just doubled {.center .hook-slide}

:::: {.shock-card}
::: {.shock-label}
Mean time to remediate
:::

:::: {.shock-row}
::: {.shock-panel}
::: {.shock-caption}
Last month
:::
::: {.shock-value}
15d
:::
:::

::: {.shock-arrow}
→
:::

::: {.shock-panel .danger}
::: {.shock-caption}
This month
:::
::: {.shock-value}
31d
:::
:::
::::

::: {.shock-subtitle}
Dashboards panic here. Better question: what happened to exposure?
:::
::::

---

## Why dashboards panic

::: {.incremental}
- Something slowed down
- The team got worse
- Leadership should worry
- Someone is already booking the meeting
:::

---

## The paradox {.center .statement-slide}

::: {.metric-big .centered}
MTTR can rise

while exposure falls
:::

::: {.callout}
Same team. Same capacity. Better prioritization.
:::

---

## MTTR is flow

Time to remediate **closed** vulnerabilities.

::: {.kicker}
It measures closure speed, not the age of what remains open.
:::

---

## MOVA is stock

::: {.metric-mid}
**Mean Open Vulnerability Age**
:::

How old is the risk we still carry?

::: {.kicker}
It measures the backlog you still own today.
:::

---

## Flow vs. stock

::: {.definition-grid}
::: {.definition-card}
::: {.eyebrow}
Flow
:::
### MTTR
How fast are we closing work?
:::

::: {.definition-card}
::: {.eyebrow}
Stock
:::
### MOVA
How old is the work still open?
:::
:::

---

## Averages hide the tail

::: {.tail-example-grid}
::: {.tail-card}
::: {.tail-number}
95
:::
::: {.tail-text}
findings at 7 days
:::
:::

::: {.tail-card}
::: {.tail-number}
5
:::
::: {.tail-text}
findings at 400 days
:::
:::

::: {.tail-card .emphasis}
::: {.tail-text .strong}
The average looks fine.
:::
::: {.tail-text}
The tail does not.
:::
:::
:::

---

## Report the bundle

::: {.signal-grid}
::: {.signal-card}
::: {.signal-name}
MTTR
:::
::: {.signal-meaning}
Closure speed
:::
:::

::: {.signal-card}
::: {.signal-name}
MOVA
:::
::: {.signal-meaning}
Exposure age
:::
:::

::: {.signal-card}
::: {.signal-name}
Open Count
:::
::: {.signal-meaning}
Backlog size
:::
:::

::: {.signal-card}
::: {.signal-name}
180+ Days
:::
::: {.signal-meaning}
Tail risk
:::
:::
:::

---

## Same data, different question

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

**MTTR**

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
mttr = (
    vulns
    .filter(pl.col("resolved_at").is_not_null())
    .with_columns(
        (pl.col("resolved_at") - pl.col("created_at"))
        .dt.total_days()
        .alias("days_to_fix")
    )
    .select(pl.col("days_to_fix").mean().alias("mttr_days"))
)
```
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

**MOVA**

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
as_of = datetime(2026, 4, 1)

mova = (
    vulns
    .filter(pl.col("resolved_at").is_null())
    .with_columns(
        (pl.lit(as_of) - pl.col("created_at"))
        .dt.total_days()
        .alias("open_age_days")
    )
    .select(pl.col("open_age_days").mean().alias("mova_days"))
)
```
:::
::::

::: {.kicker}
Same data. Different filter.
:::

---

## Why “did we fix it in time?” falls short

::: {.incremental}
- SLA views are useful
- They are weak proxies for current exposure
- They score risk after the window has passed
- MOVA shows the risk you still own today
:::

---

## Same capacity, different outcome

::: {.incremental}
- Same arrivals
- Same fix rate
- Same time horizon
- Only prioritization changes
:::

::: {.kicker}
Now watch what each metric rewards.
:::

---

## MTTR rewards recent closures

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-alt: "Line chart comparing MTTR over time for newest-first and oldest-first prioritization. Newest-first stays lower because it closes recent work first."
comparison_chart(
    mttr_compare,
    title="MTTR over time",
    subtitle="Newest-First looks better because it closes newer work first",
    y_label="MTTR (days)",
)
```

::: {.centered .muted}
On MTTR, Newest-First wins.
:::

---

## MOVA shows remaining exposure

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-alt: "Line chart comparing MOVA over time for newest-first and oldest-first prioritization. Oldest-first lowers exposure by removing old backlog first."
comparison_chart(
    mova_compare,
    title="MOVA over time",
    subtitle="Oldest-First lowers exposure because old backlog leaves first",
    y_label="MOVA (days)",
)
```

::: {.centered .muted}
On MOVA, Oldest-First wins.
:::

---

## The 180+ tail tells the truth

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-alt: "Line chart comparing the count of open vulnerabilities older than 180 days for oldest-first and newest-first prioritization."
(
    ggplot(tail_plot, aes(x="month_index", y="aged_over_180", color="strategy"))
    + geom_line(size=2.35)
    + geom_point(data=tail_labels, size=3.8)
    + geom_text(
        data=tail_labels,
        mapping=aes(label="label"),
        ha="left",
        nudge_x=0.45,
        size=10,
    )
    + scale_color_manual(values=STRATEGY_COLORS)
    + scale_x_continuous(breaks=[1, 6, 12, 18, 24])
    + coord_cartesian(xlim=(1, 26))
    + labs(
        title="180+ day backlog",
        subtitle="Newest-First keeps the tail alive",
        x="Month",
        y="Open vulnerabilities",
    )
    + chart_theme
)
```

::: {.centered .muted}
This is the risk that stays stranded.
:::

---

## End state: the tail tells the story

::: {.score-grid .tail-grid}
::: {.score-card .score-good}
::: {.score-title}
Oldest-First
:::
::: {.score-metric}
`{python} oldest_tail`
:::
::: {.score-label}
findings older than 180 days
:::
:::

::: {.score-card .score-bad}
::: {.score-title}
Newest-First
:::
::: {.score-metric}
`{python} newest_tail`
:::
::: {.score-label}
findings older than 180 days
:::
:::
:::

::: {.centered .muted}
Under Newest-First, the old risk barely moves.
:::

---

## Same data, wrong winner

```{python}
#| echo: false
(
    GT(summary_table)
    .tab_header(
        title="End state comparison",
        subtitle="Same inputs. Only prioritization changed.",
    )
    .cols_align(align="center")
    .fmt_number(columns=["MTTR (days)", "MOVA (days)"], decimals=1)
    .fmt_number(columns=["Open", "180+ days"], decimals=0)
)
```

::: {.kicker}
Read only MTTR and you pick the wrong winner.
:::

---

## When leadership sees only MTTR {auto-animate="true"}

::: {.dashboard-grid}
::: {.dashboard-card .winner}
::: {.dashboard-title}
Newest-First
:::
::: {.dashboard-value}
`{python} newest_mttr` days
:::
::: {.dashboard-label}
MTTR
:::
:::

::: {.dashboard-card}
::: {.dashboard-title}
Oldest-First
:::
::: {.dashboard-value}
`{python} oldest_mttr` days
:::
::: {.dashboard-label}
MTTR
:::
:::
:::

::: {.centered .muted}
If this is the only KPI, teams optimize for optics.
:::

---

## When leadership sees the full system {auto-animate="true"}

::: {.dashboard-grid}
::: {.dashboard-card .dashboard-detail}
::: {.dashboard-title}
Newest-First
:::
::: {.dashboard-stat}
<span>MTTR</span>
<strong>`{python} newest_mttr` days</strong>
:::
::: {.dashboard-stat}
<span>MOVA</span>
<strong>`{python} newest_mova` days</strong>
:::
::: {.dashboard-stat}
<span>Open</span>
<strong>`{python} newest_open`</strong>
:::
::: {.dashboard-stat}
<span>180+ days</span>
<strong>`{python} newest_tail`</strong>
:::
:::

::: {.dashboard-card .dashboard-detail .winner}
::: {.dashboard-title}
Oldest-First
:::
::: {.dashboard-stat}
<span>MTTR</span>
<strong>`{python} oldest_mttr` days</strong>
:::
::: {.dashboard-stat}
<span>MOVA</span>
<strong>`{python} oldest_mova` days</strong>
:::
::: {.dashboard-stat}
<span>Open</span>
<strong>`{python} oldest_open`</strong>
:::
::: {.dashboard-stat}
<span>180+ days</span>
<strong>`{python} oldest_tail`</strong>
:::
:::
:::

::: {.centered .muted}
MTTR still matters. It just cannot stand alone.
:::

---

## Read the signals together

::: {.incremental}
- MTTR up + MOVA down = backlog cleanup
- MTTR down + MOVA up = optics improve while exposure worsens
- 180+ up = risk is getting stranded in the tail
:::

---

## What to report

::: {.incremental}
- Put MTTR and MOVA on the same monthly dashboard
- Add open count and a threshold like 180+ days
- Label the MTTR window before calling a regression
- Use the bundle to explain backlog cleanup when old work closes
:::

---

## Real data, not just dashboards

Most teams already have the data. It’s usually sitting in a CSV.

::: {.incremental}
- CSVs are messy and unavoidable
- Dashboards hide the logic
- Analysts need control of the definitions
:::

---

## Own the definition in Polars

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="52%"}

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
import polars as pl

vulns = pl.read_csv("vulnerabilities.csv", try_parse_dates=True)
```

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
from datetime import datetime

as_of = datetime(2026, 4, 1)

vulns = vulns.with_columns(
    pl.when(pl.col("resolved_at").is_not_null())
    .then((pl.col("resolved_at") - pl.col("created_at")).dt.total_days())
    .otherwise(None)
    .alias("days_to_fix"),
    pl.when(pl.col("resolved_at").is_null())
    .then((pl.lit(as_of) - pl.col("created_at")).dt.total_days())
    .otherwise(None)
    .alias("open_age_days"),
)

mttr = vulns.select(pl.col("days_to_fix").mean().alias("mttr_days"))
mova = vulns.select(pl.col("open_age_days").mean().alias("mova_days"))
```
:::

::: {.column width="48%"}

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
vulns.group_by("severity").agg(
    pl.col("open_age_days").mean().alias("mova_days")
)
```
:::
::::

---

## Turn analysis into a story

Charts help people see. Tables help people decide.

---

## Plotnine for trends

Show the pattern before you explain it.

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
(
    ggplot(vulns_by_month, aes("month", "mova_days", color="team"))
    + geom_line(size=2)
    + labs(x="Month", y="MOVA (days)")
)
```

---

## Great Tables for decisions

Make the summary easy to read and hard to misread.

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
(
    GT(summary_table)
    .tab_header(title="Backlog summary")
    .fmt_number(columns=["MOVA (days)"], decimals=1)
)
```

---

## Quarto for publishing

Keep analysis, charts, and narrative together in one reproducible document.

````markdown
## Monthly Risk Review

```{{python}}
build_mova_chart(vulns)
```
````

---

## Positron for exploration

Inspect the rows behind the metric before you present it.

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
(
    vulns
    .filter(pl.col("open_age_days") > 180)
    .sort("open_age_days", descending=True)
)
```

---

## Can AI do this for me?

::: {.incremental}
- AI can draft transforms, charts, and a first pass
- You still define the metric
- Use AI to move faster; use code to own it
:::

---

## Report both {.center .statement-slide}

::: {.metric-big .centered}
MTTR measures closure speed.

MOVA measures exposure remaining.

You need both.
:::
